mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52 percent over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of declining real consumer credit growth on equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent and RSAFS declines, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes over a 2-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands aggregate demand and corporate revenue streams, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to correlate positively with 20-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below 0.05 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints trigger cost-push inflation that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when INDPRO drops below 105, or the signal's hit rate falls below 48 percent over a 3-year walk-forward validation period.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and accelerating consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that forces equity risk premiums higher, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint rise in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances, or the interaction term's predictive power becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak employment, accelerating wage growth eventually crowds out corporate margins and triggers a demand slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Accelerating payroll growth fails to precede negative 5-day forward returns when GDPC1 growth decelerates, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50 percent over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
